subject: [PATCH v3 00/16] userfaultfd: working set tracking for VM guest memory

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer Mike Rapoport suggested changing the wording of 'being interested' to a more formal phrase, such as 'of interest' or 'the app is interested', to improve clarity and readability.

Reviewer Mike Rapoport suggested that the patch should register the guest memory range with UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP and add UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_MISSING if evicted pages will need to be fetched back from storage.
reviews.raw_body:
It's pre-existing, but "being interested" sounds weird to me
Maybe let's change "being interested" to "of interest" or "the app is
interested in".

---

I'd make it

  2. Register the guest memory range with ``UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP``.
     Add ``UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_MISSING`` if evicted pages will need to be
     fetched back from storage.


-- 
Sincerely yours,
Mike.

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary: Reviewer Mike Rapoport pointed out that the patch does not apply to the current man-pages tree and requested information about the linux-man tree used for generating the patches.
reviews.raw_body:
This doesn't apply to the current man-pages tree
https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/docs/man-pages/man-pages.git

and reading raw groff hurts eyes too much.

What linux-man tree did you use to generate those?

-- 
Sincerely yours,
Mike.

2026-05-23.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for tracking the working set of VM guest memory using userfaultfd. It allows a VMM to identify cold pages and evict them to slower storage, improving performance by reducing unnecessary page faults. The approach uses a new registration mode (MODE_RWP) that marks pages as 'userfaultfd-tracked' while keeping them resident, enabling the kernel to deliver UFFD_PAGEFAULT_FLAG_RWP messages to registered handlers or auto-resolve faults in async mode.

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary: Reviewer Mike Rapoport requested that the man-page patches be sent separately from the main patch series due to confusion caused by their presence in the same thread and an apparent failure of sashiko to apply them.
reviews.raw_body:
I found that the man patches in the same thread confuse b4 review, I
suppose sashiko also failed to apply because of them.

Can you send them as a separate thread please?

2026-05-24.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for tracking the working set of VM guest memory using userfaultfd. It allows a VMM to identify cold pages and evict them to slower storage, improving performance by reducing unnecessary page faults. The approach uses a new registration mode (MODE_RWP) that marks pages as 'userfaultfd-tracked' while keeping them resident, enabling the kernel to deliver UFFD_PAGEFAULT_FLAG_RWP messages to registered handlers or auto-resolve faults in async mode.
